description: Same location but different events - should NOT match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Yoga Workshop - Advanced Flow
- Start: 2026-05-18T10:00:00
- End: 2026-05-18T12:00:00
- Location: Zen Wellness Studio
- Description: Two-hour intensive workshop on advanced vinyasa sequences

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Meditation Class
   - Start: 2026-05-18T08:00:00
   - End: 2026-05-18T09:00:00
   - Location: Zen Wellness Studio
   - Description: Morning meditation and breathing exercises

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: Pilates Session
   - Start: 2026-05-18T14:00:00
   - End: 2026-05-18T15:00:00
   - Location: Zen Wellness Studio
   - Description: Core strengthening pilates class

3. Event ID: event-003
   - Title: Coffee with Maria
   - Start: 2026-05-18T16:00:00
   - End: 2026-05-18T17:00:00
   - Location: Starbucks Downtown
   - Description: Catch up meeting

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: {"matched_event_id":null,"reasoning":"The new event 'Yoga Workshop - Advanced Flow' (10:00-12:00) does not match any existing events on May 18th. event-001 (Meditation), event-002 (Pilates), and event-003 (Coffee) occur at different times and cover different activities."}
